_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of user centered design principles.
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including their application to system architecture, data minimization, purpose limitation, storage limitation, and consent management design patterns.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-K-004 (OP, cosine: 0.502), DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.477).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.1/10 (raw 0.610). Shared vocabulary: [design, knowledge, principles]. DCWF-only terms [centered, user] vs WIDAI-only terms [application, architecture, consent, data, default, limitation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.890 (P=0.927, R=0.855). P exceeds R by 0.072 — the DCWF element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 23 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.6%). "Knowledge of user centered design principles." and "Knowledge of privacy-by-design and privacy-by-default principles, including thei" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.517 < STS 0.610 (gap -0.092). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [centered, user] and [application, architecture, consent] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 16.7%. Shared tokens: [design, knowledge, principles]. DCWF-only: [centered, user]. WIDAI-only: [application, architecture, consent, data, default, limitation].
